items.title: S&P Dow Jones Warns of Potential Frontier-Market Cut for Turkey
items.description: S&P Dow Jones Indices put Turkey on a watchlist for a potential cut to frontier-market status, joining MSCI Inc. in warning of downgrades.

items.title: US Strikes Iran and Blocks Oil Sales
items.description: The US launched airstrikes in Iran and revoked a waiver that allowed it to sell oil globally, further imperiling a peace agreement after a series of attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg

items.title: Oil Rises, Asian Stocks Steady as US Strikes Iran: Markets Wrap
items.description: Oil prices rose and Asian stocks were little changed as investors assessed the implications of renewed geopolitical tensions for energy supplies and risk assets.

items.title: Oil Climbs as US Strikes Iran Targets Following Ship Attacks
items.description: Oil jumped as the US launched a wave of strikes against Iran in retaliation for a spate of attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Exxon Sees $3.7 Billion Profit Surge From War-Driven Oil Rally
items.description: ExxonMobil Holdings Corp. said second-quarter earnings jumped by almost $4 billion as the Iran conflict boosted oil prices.

items.title: Operations Platform SnowCloud Wants to Run Resorts By iPhone
items.description: Joe Hession, CEO and co-founder of SnowCloud, and Doug Pierini, CEO of Jackson Hole Mountain Resort, discussed efforts to move customer relations including sales and guest information on one platform.

items.title: Allstate Sued by Oklahoma for Alleged Scheme to Underpay Claims
items.description: Oklahoma’s attorney general sued Allstate Corp., alleging the insurer schemed to minimize payouts to homeowners for wind and hailstorm damage in order to boost its profits.

items.title: US Judge Rejects DOJ Subpoena Over 2020 Georgia Election
items.description: A federal judge rejected an effort by the US Justice Department to obtain information on election workers in Fulton County, Georgia, related to the 2020 vote.

items.description: Options traders are increasing bets that the broader market is overestimating how much the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates this year.

items.title: Hedge Funds Shake Off Brutal March to Post Best Half Since 2021
items.description: The brutal March that battered some of the world’s biggest hedge funds is already fading into the background as the firms reported their strongest first-half results in five years.
